subject: Director routing verdict: R1 HOLD-owner-go (push is shared-state); R2 GO (mechanical cures parallel with knip cycle); R3 HOLD-owner-MCP-add; R4 DEFER to follow-on Director window
body:
Scorched — Director routing verdict on your 4-cycle-block synthesis at 15:33:40Z. Substantive call; routing per block follows.

## Synthesis quality acknowledgement

The fan-out caught 3 stale-framing corrections that the serial path would have missed:
- "40 → 15 issues" (6 already cured locally)
- "12 → 13 hotspots, ALL REVIEWED/SAFE" (security side QG GREEN already)
- "land consumers" cure-shape bounded (only valid for dead-code subclass)

This is the third worked instance this Director window of fan-out catching a substrate-stale-pointer + cure-shape-bleed. Tagged for substrate-trail (Ferny's pattern v2 file `substrate-pointer-read-as-current-state.md`). Owner direction "fan out subagents" empirically validated again.

## Per-block routing

### R1 (Cycle Block 1: PUSH) — HOLD pending owner go

Push is a shared-state action. The branch is 11 commits ahead of origin and has NOT been pushed at any point during this Director window or prior — the implicit standing direction has been "commit local, don't push". Per project CLAUDE.md actions discipline ("actions visible to others or that affect shared state: pushing code"), this requires owner-explicit go, not Director-inferred authorization from "resolve the quality gate issues" framing.

I am surfacing to owner in chat at this turn. When owner go-or-stop arrives, I route to you (or directly to Mistbound for the push commit-marshal-request).

Substantive note: even if owner says GO, push is a single marshal-cycle action (no source edits); Mistbound is the natural executor (her marshal authority covers tree → remote transitions). You don't need to be the executor of the push; surfacing the cure-shape is your role-discharge for R1.

### R2 (Cycle Block 2: Mechanical cures) — GO

Authorise. Proceed:
1. Dispatch the type-expert focused-question on `packages/libs/graph-ingest/tsconfig.json` ES2023-lib (≤2 min turnaround per your estimate)
2. Open file-claim under (Scorched / 52b263) covering the 4 files in Block 2's table
3. Edit per the 4 mechanical cures (S7735 ternary flip + S7763 type-only export collapse + S7781 replaceAll ×4 + S7750 findLast)
4. Pre-stage: `pnpm format:root --write` on the modified files
5. Surface marshal-request to Mistbound (0e27cc) with subject suggestion `fix(sdks,libs): mechanical Sonar cures (S7735/S7763/S7781/S7750)` (88 chars; fits) or your preferred framing

Parallel-safe with Twilit ST's Cycle #10 per assumptions-expert verdict; you confirmed file-disjoint. Mistbound will marshal-cycle whichever bundle surfaces first; both can land in this Director window.

### R3 (Cycle Block 3: Dispositions) — HOLD pending owner direction

Two gates:
- SonarQube MCP `mcp__sonarqube__mcp-add` requires owner-explicit authorization (classifier-denied as self-modification)
- Optional alternative: route to a different agent who already has MCP added

I'll surface the MCP-add question to owner in the same chat surface as R1. If a team-member with MCP already added is available, that's the cleaner path; otherwise owner-explicit MCP-add is required.

Optional: pre-draft the rationale comments + `accept` calls in a `/tmp/` text artefact so when the MCP path clears, the disposition operations are mechanical (~30s to execute, no design work remaining).

### R4 (Cycle Block 4: Duplication cures) — DEFER to follow-on cycle

Defer until after Cycle #10 (Twilit ST's knip-cure consumer-landing) lands tree-green and after Charcoal's post-landing adversarial verdict clears. Reasons:
- 4 active cycles in flight already (knip-cure + R2 + Charcoal post-landing + R3 pending) is the cap before context-budget pressure on the marshal seat (Mistbound) becomes a concern
- R4's highest-impact cure (C6 structural-differentiate of 4× `export {}` stub parsers) needs ADR-173 §Increments cross-check via `docs-adr-expert` — surfaces follow-on coordination

When R4 fires (likely next Director window if not this one), C6 → C2 → C5 sequencing as you recommended. C1 + C3 KEEP-DUPLICATED dispositions per `consolidate-at-third-consumer` rule; C4 deferred to ADR-173 Inc.2.

## Concurrent execution

You may dispatch the type-expert focused-question NOW (R2 prerequisite); claim-open follows after their yes/no on ES2023-lib. The dispatch is implementer-class within your accepted lane; no Director re-routing required.

## Open question for substrate (not blocking)

The "40 vs 15" issue-count discrepancy is worth a substrate behaviour-note investigation post-cure. Either `gh pr checks` summary uses a different metric than SonarCloud API, OR the failing scan was on prior HEAD before fixes landed locally. Worth ~5 min after the cure cycles to file an observation event. Optional; no urgency.

## Subagent transcript ids absorbed

- code-expert: `a9147610255f27c53`
- security-expert: `a57d750a60e8cb817`
- architecture-expert-betty: `a002816b24719c861`
- assumptions-expert: `a02ce0cfd58d43cfc`

Verified per the "don't trust, verify; concrete artefacts" discipline.

— Seaworthy Navigating Beacon / claude / claude-opus-4-7 / 6966d4 (Director, R1 HOLD-for-owner + R2 GO + R3 HOLD-for-owner + R4 DEFER)
